title: Walks
lean_code_with_comments:
/-- A single edge in a walk in the CDMG `G`, between two adjacent
vertices `v` and `w`, with explicit orientation. The three constructors
mirror the three cases in `lecture-notes/lecture_notes/graphs.tex`
def 3.4 item 1: `forward` for `v Ō¤Č[G] w` (`\tuh`), `backward` for
`v Ō¤Ą[G] w` (`\hut`, equivalent to `(w, v) Ōłł G.E`), and `bidir` for
`v Ō¤Ę[G] w` (`\huh`). Each constructor carries the underlying
adjacency proof inline. -/
inductive WalkStep (G : CDMG ╬▒) : ╬▒ ŌåÆ ╬▒ ŌåÆ Type _ where
  /-- A directed forward step `v ŌåÆ w`, witnessing `(v, w) Ōłł G.E`.
  Mirrors LN's `\tuh`. -/
  | forward {v w : ╬▒} (h : v Ō¤Č[G] w) : WalkStep G v w
  /-- A directed backward step `v ŌåÉ w`, witnessing `(w, v) Ōłł G.E`.
  Mirrors LN's `\hut`. -/
  | backward {v w : ╬▒} (h : v Ō¤Ą[G] w) : WalkStep G v w
  /-- A bidirected step `v Ōåö w`, witnessing `(v, w) Ōłł G.L`. Mirrors
  LN's `\huh`. -/
  | bidir {v w : ╬▒} (h : v Ō¤Ę[G] w) : WalkStep G v w

/-- A walk in the CDMG `G` from `v` to `w`. Inductively built from a
trivial walk `nil v : Walk G v v` (the LN's trivial single-vertex walk)
by left-consing `WalkStep`s onto a tail walk. See
`lecture-notes/lecture_notes/graphs.tex` def 3.4 item 1. The same
node may appear multiple times in the support; see `Walk.support` for
the visited-vertex list. -/
inductive Walk (G : CDMG ╬▒) : ╬▒ ŌåÆ ╬▒ ŌåÆ Type _ where
  /-- The trivial walk on a single vertex `v` (LN's "trivial walk
  consisting of a single node $v_0 \in G$"). -/
  | nil (v : ╬▒) : Walk G v v
  /-- Extend a walk by left-consing a leading edge step. -/
  | cons {v w u : ╬▒} (s : WalkStep G v w) (p : Walk G w u) : Walk G v u

/-- The orientation-reversed version of a `WalkStep`. Swaps `forward
Ōåö backward` (a definitional identity on the underlying `G.E`
membership) and conjugates `bidir` by `G.L_symm` from def 3.1. Used by
`Walk.reverse`. -/
def reverse : {v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ WalkStep G v w ŌåÆ WalkStep G w v
  | _, _, .forward h  => .backward h
  | _, _, .backward h => .forward h
  | _, _, .bidir h    => .bidir (G.L_symm h)

/-- The *length* of a walk: the number of edges (`WalkStep`s) it
contains. This is the LN's parameter `n` in `v = v_0, ŌĆ”, v_n = w`. -/
def length : {v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ Walk G v w ŌåÆ ŌäĢ
  | _, _, .nil _    => 0
  | _, _, .cons _ p => p.length + 1

/-- The list of vertices visited by a walk, in order
(`v_0, v_1, ŌĆ”, v_n` in the LN's notation). It has length
`p.length + 1`; see `Walk.support_length`. -/
def support : {v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ Walk G v w ŌåÆ List ╬▒
  | _, _, .nil v    => [v]
  | v, _, .cons _ p => v :: p.support

/-- The first `WalkStep` of a walk, if any: `none` on the trivial
walk, `some Ō¤©v, w, sŌ¤®` on `cons s _` where `s : WalkStep G v w`.
Packaged as a dependent pair so the step's endpoints remain
inspectable. -/
def firstStep? : {v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ Walk G v w ŌåÆ Option (╬Ż' (v' w' : ╬▒), WalkStep G v' w')
  | _, _, .nil _    => none
  | _, _, .cons s _ => some Ō¤©_, _, sŌ¤®

/-- The last `WalkStep` of a walk, if any. Trivial walk ŌåÆ `none`;
single-step walk ŌåÆ `some` of that step; longer walk ŌåÆ recurse into
the tail. -/
def lastStep? : {v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ Walk G v w ŌåÆ Option (╬Ż' (v' w' : ╬▒), WalkStep G v' w')
  | _, _, .nil _              => none
  | _, _, .cons s (.nil _)    => some Ō¤©_, _, sŌ¤®
  | _, _, .cons _ (.cons s p) => lastStep? (.cons s p)

/-- The concatenation of two walks, sharing a common endpoint.
Mathlib's `SimpleGraph.Walk.append` is the structural precedent. -/
def append : {u v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ Walk G u v ŌåÆ Walk G v w ŌåÆ Walk G u w
  | _, _, _, .nil _,    q => q
  | _, _, _, .cons s p, q => .cons s (p.append q)

/-- The walk in reverse. Defined by `(nil v).reverse = nil v` and
`(cons s p).reverse = p.reverse.append (cons s.reverse (nil v))`. -/
def reverse : {v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ Walk G v w ŌåÆ Walk G w v
  | _, _, .nil v    => .nil v
  | v, _, .cons s p => p.reverse.append (.cons s.reverse (.nil v))

/-- A *directed walk*: every step is a `forward` step. Equivalent
to the LN's "all arrowheads point in the direction of $w$ and
there are no arrowheads pointing back" condition. The trivial walk
is directed vacuously. -/
def IsDirected : {v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ Walk G v w ŌåÆ Prop
  | _, _, .nil _              => True
  | _, _, .cons (.forward _) p => p.IsDirected
  | _, _, .cons (.backward _) _ => False
  | _, _, .cons (.bidir _) _    => False

/-- A *bidirected walk*: every step is a `bidir` step. The trivial
walk is bidirected vacuously. -/
def IsBidirected : {v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ Walk G v w ŌåÆ Prop
  | _, _, .nil _              => True
  | _, _, .cons (.bidir _) p   => p.IsBidirected
  | _, _, .cons (.forward _) _ => False
  | _, _, .cons (.backward _) _ => False

/-- A *collider walk* in `G` from `v` to `w`: the LN shape
$v_0 \suh v_1 \huh \cdots \huh v_{n-1} \hus v_n$. The trivial walk
is a collider walk vacuously; a single-step walk is a collider
walk iff that step is bidirected (see the design-choice block
above for the $n = 1$ interpretation). -/
def IsCollider : {v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ Walk G v w ŌåÆ Prop
  | _, _, .nil _              => True
  | _, _, .cons s (.nil _)    => s.IsBidir
  | _, _, .cons s (.cons s' p) =>
      s.HasArrowheadAtTarget Ōł¦ (Walk.cons s' p).IsColliderInner

/-- A *path* in `G`: a walk whose visited-vertex sequence has no
repeats. Equivalent to `List.Nodup` on `Walk.support`. -/
def IsPath {v w : ╬▒} (ŽĆ : Walk G v w) : Prop := ŽĆ.support.Nodup

/-- The walk is *into $v_0$* (LN item 1 prose): the first
`WalkStep` is `backward` or `bidir`, i.e. it has an arrowhead at
the walk's start vertex. The trivial walk is *not* into its start
vertex (it has no first edge). -/
def IntoStart : {v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ Walk G v w ŌåÆ Prop
  | _, _, .nil _              => False
  | _, _, .cons s _           => s.HasArrowheadAtSource

/-- The walk is *out of $v_0$* (LN item 1 prose): the first
`WalkStep` is `forward`, i.e. a directed edge originating at
$v_0$. The trivial walk is not out of its start vertex. -/
def OutOfStart : {v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ Walk G v w ŌåÆ Prop
  | _, _, .nil _              => False
  | _, _, .cons (.forward _) _ => True
  | _, _, .cons (.backward _) _ => False
  | _, _, .cons (.bidir _) _    => False

/-- The walk is *into $v_n$* (LN item 1 prose): the last
`WalkStep` has an arrowhead at the walk's end vertex
(`HasArrowheadAtTarget`). The trivial walk is not into its end
vertex (no last edge). -/
def IntoEnd : {v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ Walk G v w ŌåÆ Prop
  | _, _, .nil _              => False
  | _, _, .cons s (.nil _)    => s.HasArrowheadAtTarget
  | _, _, .cons _ (.cons s p) => IntoEnd (.cons s p)

/-- The walk is *out of $v_n$* (LN item 1 prose): the last
`WalkStep` is `backward`, i.e. a directed edge with $v_n$ as the
tail. The trivial walk is not out of its end vertex. -/
def OutOfEnd : {v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ Walk G v w ŌåÆ Prop
  | _, _, .nil _              => False
  | _, _, .cons s (.nil _)    =>
      -- Single-step walk: this step is the last; it must be backward.
      match s with
      | .backward _ => True
      | _           => False
  | _, _, .cons _ (.cons s p) => OutOfEnd (.cons s p)

/-- A walk in which *every step* is a `backward` step (the LN's
`\hut`). The trivial walk is vacuously `IsAllBackward`. The LN's
"the subwalk $v_0 \hut \cdots v_{k-1}$ is a directed walk from
$v_{k-1}$ to $v_0$" (def 3.4 item 6, left arm of a bifurcation)
is equivalent to this predicate on the left arm. -/
def IsAllBackward : {v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ Walk G v w ŌåÆ Prop
  | _, _, .nil _               => True
  | _, _, .cons (.backward _) p => p.IsAllBackward
  | _, _, .cons (.forward _) _  => False
  | _, _, .cons (.bidir _) _    => False

/-- Witness that a walk `ŽĆ : Walk G v w` is a bifurcation: an
explicit decomposition into a left arm (all `backward` steps), a
hinge step (with an arrowhead at its source, i.e. `backward` or
`bidir`), and a right arm (all `forward` steps), matching LN
def 3.4 item 6's shape
$v_0 \hut \cdots \hut v_{k-1} \hus v_k \tuh \cdots \tuh v_n$. -/
structure BifurcationWitness {v w : ╬▒} (ŽĆ : Walk G v w) : Type _ where
  /-- The LN's $v_{k-1}$: end of the left arm, start of the hinge. -/
  m : ╬▒
  /-- The LN's $v_k$: end of the hinge, start of the right arm. -/
  m' : ╬▒
  /-- The left arm $v_0 \hut \cdots \hut v_{k-1}$. Has length
  $k - 1$; may be the trivial walk `nil v` if $k = 1$. -/
  leftArm : Walk G v m
  /-- The hinge step $v_{k-1} \hus v_k$ -- either `backward`
  (LN's `\hut`, giving a source) or `bidir` (LN's `\huh`, no
  source). -/
  hinge : WalkStep G m m'
  /-- The right arm $v_k \tuh \cdots \tuh v_n$. Has length
  $n - k$; may be the trivial walk `nil w` if $k = n$. -/
  rightArm : Walk G m' w
  /-- The walk `ŽĆ` decomposes as `leftArm` followed by the hinge
  step followed by `rightArm`. -/
  decompose : ŽĆ = leftArm.append (Walk.cons hinge rightArm)
  /-- Every step of the left arm is a `backward` step
  (LN: "$v_0 \hut \cdots \hut v_{k-1}$"). -/
  leftBackward : leftArm.IsAllBackward
  /-- The hinge step has an arrowhead at its source endpoint,
  i.e. it is `backward` or `bidir` (LN: "$v_{k-1} \hus v_k$"). -/
  hingeIntoSource : hinge.HasArrowheadAtSource
  /-- Every step of the right arm is a `forward` step
  (LN: "$v_k \tuh \cdots \tuh v_n$"). -/
  rightDirected : rightArm.IsDirected

/-- A walk `ŽĆ : Walk G v w` is a *bifurcation* between $v$ and $w$
in $G$ (LN def 3.4 item 6) iff $v \ne w$, both endnodes appear
exactly once in `ŽĆ.support`, and `ŽĆ` admits a witness
decomposition into a left arm (all `backward`), a hinge step
(`backward` or `bidir`), and a right arm (all `forward`). -/
def IsBifurcation {v w : ╬▒} (ŽĆ : Walk G v w) : Prop :=
  v ŌēĀ w Ōł¦
  v Ōłē ŽĆ.support.tail Ōł¦
  w Ōłē ŽĆ.support.dropLast Ōł¦
  Nonempty (BifurcationWitness ŽĆ)

/-- The *source* of a bifurcation in the LN def 3.4 item 6 sense:
`some bw.m'` if the chosen witness's hinge is `backward` (the
LN's "$v_{k-1} \hut v_k$" directed-hinge case, where the source
is $v_k$), or `none` if it is `bidir` (LN's "$v_{k-1} \huh v_k$"
bidirected-hinge case, where the LN does not assign a source).
The witness is selected by `Classical.choice` from the
`Nonempty (BifurcationWitness ŽĆ)` clause of `IsBifurcation`; see
the module docstring for the well-definedness caveat. -/
noncomputable def bifurcationSource {v w : ╬▒} (ŽĆ : Walk G v w)
    (hb : ŽĆ.IsBifurcation) : Option ╬▒ :=
  let bw := hb.2.2.2.some
  match bw.hinge with
  | .backward _ => some bw.m'
  | _           => none
lean_code_without_comments:
inductive WalkStep (G : CDMG ╬▒) : ╬▒ ŌåÆ ╬▒ ŌåÆ Type _ where

  | forward {v w : ╬▒} (h : v Ō¤Č[G] w) : WalkStep G v w

  | backward {v w : ╬▒} (h : v Ō¤Ą[G] w) : WalkStep G v w

  | bidir {v w : ╬▒} (h : v Ō¤Ę[G] w) : WalkStep G v w

inductive Walk (G : CDMG ╬▒) : ╬▒ ŌåÆ ╬▒ ŌåÆ Type _ where

  | nil (v : ╬▒) : Walk G v v

  | cons {v w u : ╬▒} (s : WalkStep G v w) (p : Walk G w u) : Walk G v u

def reverse : {v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ WalkStep G v w ŌåÆ WalkStep G w v
  | _, _, .forward h  => .backward h
  | _, _, .backward h => .forward h
  | _, _, .bidir h    => .bidir (G.L_symm h)

def length : {v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ Walk G v w ŌåÆ ŌäĢ
  | _, _, .nil _    => 0
  | _, _, .cons _ p => p.length + 1

def support : {v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ Walk G v w ŌåÆ List ╬▒
  | _, _, .nil v    => [v]
  | v, _, .cons _ p => v :: p.support

def firstStep? : {v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ Walk G v w ŌåÆ Option (╬Ż' (v' w' : ╬▒), WalkStep G v' w')
  | _, _, .nil _    => none
  | _, _, .cons s _ => some Ō¤©_, _, sŌ¤®

def lastStep? : {v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ Walk G v w ŌåÆ Option (╬Ż' (v' w' : ╬▒), WalkStep G v' w')
  | _, _, .nil _              => none
  | _, _, .cons s (.nil _)    => some Ō¤©_, _, sŌ¤®
  | _, _, .cons _ (.cons s p) => lastStep? (.cons s p)

def append : {u v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ Walk G u v ŌåÆ Walk G v w ŌåÆ Walk G u w
  | _, _, _, .nil _,    q => q
  | _, _, _, .cons s p, q => .cons s (p.append q)

def reverse : {v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ Walk G v w ŌåÆ Walk G w v
  | _, _, .nil v    => .nil v
  | v, _, .cons s p => p.reverse.append (.cons s.reverse (.nil v))

def IsDirected : {v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ Walk G v w ŌåÆ Prop
  | _, _, .nil _              => True
  | _, _, .cons (.forward _) p => p.IsDirected
  | _, _, .cons (.backward _) _ => False
  | _, _, .cons (.bidir _) _    => False

def IsBidirected : {v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ Walk G v w ŌåÆ Prop
  | _, _, .nil _              => True
  | _, _, .cons (.bidir _) p   => p.IsBidirected
  | _, _, .cons (.forward _) _ => False
  | _, _, .cons (.backward _) _ => False

def IsCollider : {v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ Walk G v w ŌåÆ Prop
  | _, _, .nil _              => True
  | _, _, .cons s (.nil _)    => s.IsBidir
  | _, _, .cons s (.cons s' p) =>
      s.HasArrowheadAtTarget Ōł¦ (Walk.cons s' p).IsColliderInner

def IsPath {v w : ╬▒} (ŽĆ : Walk G v w) : Prop := ŽĆ.support.Nodup

def IntoStart : {v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ Walk G v w ŌåÆ Prop
  | _, _, .nil _              => False
  | _, _, .cons s _           => s.HasArrowheadAtSource

def OutOfStart : {v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ Walk G v w ŌåÆ Prop
  | _, _, .nil _              => False
  | _, _, .cons (.forward _) _ => True
  | _, _, .cons (.backward _) _ => False
  | _, _, .cons (.bidir _) _    => False

def IntoEnd : {v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ Walk G v w ŌåÆ Prop
  | _, _, .nil _              => False
  | _, _, .cons s (.nil _)    => s.HasArrowheadAtTarget
  | _, _, .cons _ (.cons s p) => IntoEnd (.cons s p)

def OutOfEnd : {v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ Walk G v w ŌåÆ Prop
  | _, _, .nil _              => False
  | _, _, .cons s (.nil _)    =>

      match s with
      | .backward _ => True
      | _           => False
  | _, _, .cons _ (.cons s p) => OutOfEnd (.cons s p)

def IsAllBackward : {v w : ╬▒} ŌåÆ Walk G v w ŌåÆ Prop
  | _, _, .nil _               => True
  | _, _, .cons (.backward _) p => p.IsAllBackward
  | _, _, .cons (.forward _) _  => False
  | _, _, .cons (.bidir _) _    => False

structure BifurcationWitness {v w : ╬▒} (ŽĆ : Walk G v w) : Type _ where

  m : ╬▒

  m' : ╬▒

  leftArm : Walk G v m

  hinge : WalkStep G m m'

  rightArm : Walk G m' w

  decompose : ŽĆ = leftArm.append (Walk.cons hinge rightArm)

  leftBackward : leftArm.IsAllBackward

  hingeIntoSource : hinge.HasArrowheadAtSource

  rightDirected : rightArm.IsDirected

def IsBifurcation {v w : ╬▒} (ŽĆ : Walk G v w) : Prop :=
  v ŌēĀ w Ōł¦
  v Ōłē ŽĆ.support.tail Ōł¦
  w Ōłē ŽĆ.support.dropLast Ōł¦
  Nonempty (BifurcationWitness ŽĆ)

noncomputable def bifurcationSource {v w : ╬▒} (ŽĆ : Walk G v w)
    (hb : ŽĆ.IsBifurcation) : Option ╬▒ :=
  let bw := hb.2.2.2.some
  match bw.hinge with
  | .backward _ => some bw.m'
  | _           => none
lean_explanation:
The ambient binder `╬▒ : Type*` is the carrier of vertices; every declaration here is parameterised over a `G : CDMG ╬▒`, the bundled graph from def 3.1. A walk in `G` from `v` to `w` is, in the LN, an alternating sequence of vertices and edges; in Lean we encode this in two inductive layers. `WalkStep G v w : Type _` is a single edge between adjacent vertices `v` and `w`, with three constructors `forward`, `backward`, `bidir` matching the LN's three orientation cases ($\tuh$, $\hut$, $\huh$). Each constructor carries the underlying adjacency proof inline ŌĆö `v Ō¤Č[G] w` for `forward`, `v Ō¤Ą[G] w` for `backward`, `v Ō¤Ę[G] w` for `bidir` ŌĆö so the existence of a walk certifies the existence of its edges. `Walk G v w : Type _` is then the umbrella walk, an inductive indexed by the endpoint pair, built by left-consing `WalkStep`s onto a trivial `nil v : Walk G v v` base case (the LN's "trivial walk consisting of a single node $v_0 \in G$").

The data layer ships a small API mirroring Mathlib's `SimpleGraph.Walk`: `length` (the LN's parameter $n$), `support` (the visited-vertex list $v_0, v_1, \ldots, v_n$), `firstStep?` and `lastStep?` (`Option`-valued head/tail accessors), `append` for concatenation, `reverse` for orientation reversal, and `WalkStep.reverse` for the per-step orientation flip. The first/last step accessors return an `Option (╬Ż' v' w', WalkStep G v' w')` ŌĆö the dependent-pair `PSigma` (notation `╬Ż'`) packages the step together with both of its endpoints, since those endpoints are not fixed by the walk's `v`/`w` indices.

On top of this data layer sit eight `Prop`-valued predicates carving out the LN's walk varieties. `IsDirected`, `IsBidirected`, `IsCollider`, and `IsPath` are the LN's items 2ŌĆō5; the first three are defined by structural recursion on the walk (each step must use the right constructor), while `IsPath` is defined as `support.Nodup`, reusing Mathlib's `List.Nodup` API rather than rolling its own per-step recursion. `IntoStart`, `OutOfStart`, `IntoEnd`, and `OutOfEnd` capture item 1's "into / out of $v_0$ / $v_n$" prose by inspecting the first or last `WalkStep`'s constructor.

The bifurcation layer (LN item 6) is the structurally trickiest piece, since the LN's prose decomposes a bifurcation at an existentially-quantified hinge index $k$. We encode this as a `Type _`-valued `BifurcationWitness` structure that names the three pieces directly ŌĆö a left arm (all `backward`, matching the LN's $v_0 \hut \cdots \hut v_{k-1}$), a hinge step (with an arrowhead at its source endpoint, the LN's $\hus$), and a right arm (all `forward`, the LN's $\tuh \cdots \tuh v_n$) ŌĆö plus a `decompose` equation tying the three pieces back to the original walk. The `Prop`-valued predicate `IsBifurcation` bundles the LN's endpoint constraints ($v \ne w$, both endnodes exactly once) with `Nonempty (BifurcationWitness ŽĆ)`, lifting the witness structure into `Prop`. The `bifurcationSource` extractor returns `some v_k` when the chosen witness's hinge is `backward` (the LN's directed-hinge "source $v_k$" case) and `none` when it is `bidir`; it is `noncomputable` because `Nonempty.some` invokes `Classical.choice`.
design_choices:
**Walks are data, not propositions.** Making `Walk` a `Type _` rather than a `Prop` is the most consequential choice in the file. A propositional "there exists a walk" relation supports only existential reasoning, but downstream chapters concatenate, reverse, induct on, and extract sub-walks of *named* walks ŌĆö none of which survives a `Prop`-valued definition. Existential phrasings remain cheap on top: `Ōłā ŽĆ : Walk G v w, ŽĆ.IsDirected` matches the LN's existential shape used in def 3.5 for ancestors and descendants.

**Three step constructors, not a single constructor with an orientation enum.** A single-constructor `WalkStep` carrying an `Orientation` field would force a `match` on the enum at every use site ŌĆö exactly the case analysis the LN itself performs in prose. Mirroring the three orientations as three constructors lets downstream pattern matches read like the LN's own case splits, and lets `WalkStep.reverse` collapse two of three cases to `rfl` (the `forward Ōåö backward` swap is an argument swap on the underlying $(v, w) \in G.E$ membership; only `bidir Ōåö bidir` needs `G.L_symm`).

**`nil` is `True` for the umbrella predicates, `False` for the prose predicates.** For `IsDirected` / `IsBidirected` / `IsCollider` / `IsPath`, the trivial walk satisfies the predicate vacuously ŌĆö the LN's "for some $n \ge 0$" admits $n = 0$. This is load-bearing for def 3.5, which needs $v \in \mathrm{Anc}^G(v)$ via the trivial directed walk. For the four "into / out of" prose predicates, the LN references a specific edge $a_0$ or $a_{n-1}$ that doesn't exist on the trivial walk, so they default to `False`.

**Single-step collider walks must be bidirected.** Reading the LN's general formula at $n = 1$ collapses to a single edge that is simultaneously $\suh$ and $\hus$, i.e. bidirected. The LN's parenthetical "for $n = 1$ this reads $v \sus w$" is slightly looser, but the strict reading is the only one compatible with the general formula's $n$-instantiation, and downstream uses (def 3.15 / 3.17, fci.tex) only ever invoke collider walks via the general shape.

**Bifurcation by witness structure, not by numeric hinge index.** The LN existentially quantifies the hinge position $k$. A natural alternative ŌĆö `takeSteps` / `dropSteps` operators and a predicate of the form "there exists $k$ such that..." ŌĆö has fiddly off-by-one arithmetic, and forces `bifurcationSource` to extract the hinge by index arithmetic. Naming the three pieces (left arm, hinge step, right arm) directly in a `Type _`-valued witness sidesteps this: the hinge becomes a `WalkStep` field, its constructor a pure pattern-match away, and the encoding mirrors the LN's downstream usage where claim 3.5 and chapter 16 manipulate the arms separately. The LN's $k$ remains recoverable as `1 + leftArm.length` if needed.

**"Both endnodes exactly once" via `support.tail` / `support.dropLast`, not `List.count`.** The natural phrasing `support.count v = 1` requires `[DecidableEq ╬▒]`, but def 3.1's `CDMG ╬▒` is parameterised over an arbitrary vertex type ŌĆö chapter 4 onwards uses real-valued nodes, where decidable equality is unavailable. The decidability-free reading `v Ōłē support.tail Ōł¦ w Ōłē support.dropLast` captures the same content: $v$ doesn't appear past position 0 (where it sits as `support.head`), and $w$ doesn't appear before position $n$.
